title: The RAG Pipeline Flow
concept: RAG flow: embed user query -> search vector database -> retrieve top document chunks -> append chunks to prompt -> LLM reads and generates answer.

exercises.prompt: Before we dive into the pipeline flow, what does the acronym RAG stand for?
exercises.explain: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is a technique that retrieves external data (like documents from a database) to help the LLM generate more accurate answers.

cards.title: How AI Finds the Facts
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how an AI chatbot can answer specific questions about your personal accounts or private company documents without making things up? It follows a special step-by-step pipeline to find the facts before it speaks!

cards.title: The RAG Pipeline Flow
cards.body: The RAG Pipeline is the step-by-step path a user's question takes to get a smart, factual answer. It connects your question to a vector database to find relevant documents, and then hands those documents to the LLM so it doesn't have to guess the answer.

cards.title: The Library Analogy
cards.body: Imagine you are writing a history report. Instead of writing from memory, you run to the library, grab the three best books on the topic, lay them open on your desk, and use them to write your report. The vector database is your library, the retrieved chunks are your open books, and you are the LLM writing the final report!

cards.title: RAG in Action
cards.body: When you ask a customer support AI: "When does my package arrive?", the RAG pipeline converts your question into a vector, searches the company database for your tracking ID, retrieves the shipping update, pastes it into a prompt, and the LLM says: "Your package is arriving tomorrow at 3 PM!"

cards.title: The LLM is Not a Search Engine
cards.body: People think the LLM directly searches the internet or its memory for everything you ask. Actually, in a RAG system, the LLM is kept in the dark until a search engine first finds the exact document chunks it needs to read.

cards.title: Standard vs. RAG Flow
cards.body: Let's compare how a standard LLM answers a question versus how an LLM powered by a RAG pipeline flow answers it.

left.text: Guesses the answer using only what it learned during training. Can hallucinate fake details.

right.text: Reads actual retrieved document chunks to generate a verified, factual response.

exercises.prompt: What does the RAG pipeline do immediately after retrieving the top document chunks from the database?

options.text: It deletes the question and only sends the chunks.
It combines the chunks and the original question into a single prompt.
It trains the LLM on the chunks overnight.
It emails the chunks to the user.

exercises.explain: In the RAG pipeline, the retrieved document chunks are pasted directly into the prompt along with the user's original question so the LLM has all the facts in front of it.

exercises.prompt: In a RAG pipeline, the LLM reads the retrieved document chunks to generate its final response. Is this statement true or false?
exercises.explain: True! The LLM doesn't search the database itself. The pipeline retrieves the document chunks first and feeds them to the LLM in the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the first step of the RAG flow!
exercises.sentence: To search the database, the pipeline first turns the user's query into a ________ embedding.
exercises.explain: The RAG pipeline must convert the user's plain text query into a vector (number representation) so it can search the vector database for matching documents.

exercises.prompt: Identify which chatbot is successfully using a RAG pipeline flow to answer a user asking: "What did I buy on Tuesday?"

options.text: AI A: "I think you bought some shoes or maybe a shirt last week, let me guess!"
AI B: "Looking at your database records: on Tuesday you purchased a Blue Cotton T-Shirt for $20."
AI C: "I cannot search databases, please upload a photo of your receipt."

exercises.explain: AI B uses the RAG pipeline. It first retrieves the specific receipt details from the database and uses them to construct an accurate answer, whereas AI A is just guessing from memory.

exercises.prompt: A user asks your AI chatbot: "What is the store's return policy?" Arrange the key steps of the RAG pipeline in the correct sequence from start to finish!

options.text: Embed Query -> Search Vector DB -> Retrieve Chunks -> Combine into Prompt -> LLM Generates Answer
LLM Generates Answer -> Embed Query -> Search Vector DB -> Combine into Prompt
Search Vector DB -> LLM Generates Answer -> Embed Query -> Retrieve Chunks
Embed Query -> Retrieve Chunks -> LLM Generates Answer -> Search Vector DB

exercises.explain: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline operates by first converting the user's question into an embedding, searching the vector database for matching documents, combining those documents with the original question into a prompt, and finally sending it to the LLM to generate the final answer.
